trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Canada exports diamonds, aircraft parts, agricultural goods to Israel. Israel exports medical equipment, high-tech products, software, and precision instruments to Canada. A Canada-Israel FTA has existed since 1997.

diplomatic.summary: Historically strong relations rooted in a large Jewish-Canadian community and shared democratic values. Canada has been a strong Israel supporter at the UN historically, though under Trudeau government Canada took a more balanced approach on Palestinian issues. The Gaza conflict has complicated relations domestically.
recent_incidents: Canada voted for UN ceasefire resolution in Gaza (2024)
Domestic political debates over arms exports to Israel

military.summary: Defense cooperation exists through equipment sales and technology sharing. Canada has historically supplied components used in Israeli defense systems. The Gaza conflict has put pressure on arms export licenses.

financial.summary: Strong bilateral investment. Israeli tech companies have significant Canadian operations (especially in Toronto and Montreal tech hubs). Canadian investors participate in Israeli startup ecosystem. CIFTA facilitates investment.

energy.summary: Limited direct energy trade. Some cooperation on clean energy technology. Canada exports small volumes of natural gas derivatives.

scientific.summary: Very active scientific cooperation in AI, cybersecurity, life sciences, and cleantech. Universities of Toronto, Waterloo, and Montreal collaborate with Weizmann Institute, Hebrew University, and Technion. Strong academic ties.
